blockingIssues.description: FirecrackerSandbox is a STUB - all methods contain 'STUB: In real implementation, this would...' comments and return mock responses. The feature requires 'Firecracker microVM provisioned per task' with '<125ms startup time' and 'hardware virtualization isolation' but none of this is implemented. Commit 2fe7084 is for hybrid executor, not firecracker.

CRITICAL BUG at line 373: start() extracts container_id from docker run output but never stores it in self.container_id. All subsequent operations (execute, read_file, write_file, is_running, stop) fail because container_id is always None. Fix: assign container_id to self.container_id before Ok(()) in start().

Two blocking bugs: (1) Cache memory leak at line 445 - is_cached() returns false for expired entries but never removes them from HashMap, causing unbounded memory growth. (2) get_lease_ttl() at line 515 is stubbed to return None, preventing automatic credential rotation monitoring.

Cedar policy engine is not integrated with existing tool execution flow - executor.rs still uses PermissionChecker with PermissionTier, not CedarPolicyEngine. Also duplicate ToolOperation enum exists in both cedar_policy.rs (line 74) and opa_policy.rs causing maintenance issues.

Validation check bug at line 370: request_credential() requires 'read' in scope.allowed_operations but git scopes only have 'push/pull/fetch' and LLM scopes only have 'chat/embed'. This breaks credential requests for non-read operations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that .factory/services.yaml exists at repo root (not ~/.factory/) - workers attempted to read from wrong path.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple workers tried to read ~/.factory/services.yaml but it actually exists at /Users/ludwigjonsson/Projects/swell/.factory/services.yaml

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add boundary rule: When a feature's expected behavior cannot be met by existing stub code (e.g., Firecracker requiring KVM/hardware virtualization), workers must report this as incomplete rather than marking the stub as complete.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tools-firecracker-sandbox worker treated a stub marked '(future)' in architecture.md as complete implementation

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Standardize skill names - features reference 'rust-worker' skill but actual skill is 'Rust'. Document the canonical skill names.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tools-permission-tiers, tools-shell-exec, tools-search reference non-existent 'rust-worker' skill

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that when implementing policy engines (Cedar, OPA), ToolOperation enum should be shared, not duplicated. Create a shared types module for cross-cutting enums.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cedar_policy.rs and opa_policy.rs both define ToolOperation with identical variants

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Firecracker sandbox is explicitly marked as '(future)' and requires KVM/bare-metal. Clarify that it's a stub placeholder and cannot be fully implemented in macOS/container environments.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: plan/research_documents/Tools and Runtime Control Spec.md states 'Firecracker requires direct KVM access, which means bare-metal hosts or nested virtualization. It won't run inside standard Kubernetes pods.'

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker noted rust-worker skill doesn't exist
rejectedObservations.reason: This is covered by the systematic skill name mismatch suggestion above
